backend/main.mo: Main actor for the MediTrack backend, handling medical records CRUD, user profile management, access control via authorization mixins, and blob storage for file uploads.
backend/migration.mo: Migration module that upgrades the actor state by adding `temporaryFiles` and `uploadedFiles` maps to the existing user records structure.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report mapping the 'Upload Record' UI feature (OCR extraction, drag-and-drop, form pre-population) to its relevant frontend files and component dependencies.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON document containing one-line summaries of all frontend source files, describing their purpose within the MediTrack React application.
frontend/index.css: Tailwind base stylesheet defining OKLCH CSS custom properties for the light and dark color themes used throughout the app.
frontend/index.html: Minimal HTML entry point that mounts the React app into a `#root` div and loads the main TypeScript module.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Root React component that sets up TanStack Router with protected layout routes, redirecting unauthenticated users to `/login`, and renders a global Toaster.
frontend/src/components/AnalysisDashboar: Category-tabbed dashboard component that renders progression charts and trend analysis for medical record categories that have at least two records.
frontend/src/components/AuthGuard.tsx: Authentication guard component that shows a loading spinner while initializing and displays a profile setup modal if the authenticated user has no profile yet.
frontend/src/components/CategoryFieldSet: Renders category-specific form fields for medical record types, showing numeric input groups for lab results (CBC, LFT, Cholesterol, BloodSugar, BloodPressure) and a description/severity/treatment textarea+select layout for GeneralAilments.
frontend/src/components/DashboardSidebar: Sticky sidebar component displaying total record count, quick navigation links (Timeline, Analysis, Add Record), and a per-category record breakdown with icons and counts fetched via useGetAllRecords.
frontend/src/components/FileUploadZone.t: Drag-and-drop file upload zone that accepts PDF, PNG, and JPEG files up to 5 MB, showing a preview of the selected file with a clear button and inline validation error messages.
frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx: Root layout component wrapping all pages with a Navigation header, AuthGuard, optional DashboardSidebar (shown when authenticated with records), an Outlet for page content, and a footer.
frontend/src/components/LoginButton.tsx: Button component that handles Internet Identity login and logout, showing a loading spinner during authentication and clearing the React Query cache on logout.
frontend/src/components/MedicalRecordFor: Form for adding a new medical record, allowing the user to select a category and date, fill in category-specific fields via CategoryFieldSet, validate inputs, and submit via the useAddMedicalRecord mutation with toast feedback.
frontend/src/components/Navigation.tsx: Sticky top navigation bar with the MediTrack logo, authenticated desktop and mobile nav links (Add Record, Timeline, Analysis), a user identity indicator, and a mobile hamburger menu toggle.
frontend/src/components/ProfileSetupModa: A modal dialog that prompts new users to enter their name to set up a health profile, using the `useSaveCallerUserProfile` mutation and blocking dismissal until submitted.
frontend/src/components/ProgressionChart: A Recharts-based line chart component that visualizes medical metric trends over time, with reference range lines and a custom tooltip showing in/out-of-range status.
frontend/src/components/TimelineCard.tsx: A collapsible card component that displays a single medical record with a summary line, expandable metric details with reference-range indicators, and a delete confirmation dialog.
frontend/src/components/TimelineView.tsx: A tabbed list view that fetches all medical records and renders them filtered by record type using `TimelineCard`, with loading skeletons and empty states.
frontend/src/components/TrendSummary.tsx: A grid component that displays trend badges and directional icons for a list of `MetricTrend` objects, showing first-to-latest value changes and out-of-range warnings.
frontend/src/components/UploadRecordForm: A form component for uploading medical record files that runs OCR extraction to pre-populate category-specific fields, tracks upload/extraction progress, and submits the record via `useAddMedicalRecord`.
frontend/src/components/UserIdentityIndi: Renders a dropdown menu showing the authenticated user's name/principal with a logout option that clears identity and redirects to the login page.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Custom hook that creates and caches an ICP backend actor (authenticated or anonymous) using React Query, and invalidates dependent queries whenever the actor changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides an InternetIdentityProvider and useInternetIdentity hook for managing ICP Internet Identity authentication state, including login, logout, and identity persistence via AuthClient.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Defines React Query hooks for all backend data operations including fetching/adding/deleting medical records, managing user profiles, and uploading/deleting files.
frontend/src/index.css: Global stylesheet that defines Tailwind base/component/utility layers, CSS custom properties for a medical teal color palette in light and dark modes, and utility component classes.
frontend/src/lib/ocrExtraction.ts: Utility library that extracts structured medical data (dates and test values like CBC, LFT, cholesterol, blood sugar, blood pressure) from raw OCR text using regex patterns.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that mounts the React app wrapped with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider.
frontend/src/pages/AddRecordPage.tsx: Page component that provides a tabbed interface for adding medical records either via manual form entry or by uploading and auto-extracting data from a lab report file.
frontend/src/pages/AnalysisPage.tsx: Page component that renders the Progression Analysis view with a header and the AnalysisDashboard component.
frontend/src/pages/HomePage.tsx: Landing page displaying a hero section, summary stats, and feature cards linking to Add Record, Timeline, and Analysis pages.
frontend/src/pages/LoginPage.tsx: Login page that handles Internet Identity authentication, shows a loading state during initialization, and redirects authenticated users to home.
frontend/src/pages/TimelinePage.tsx: Page component that renders the Medical Timeline view with a header and the TimelineView component.
frontend/src/types/fileUpload.ts: TypeScript interfaces and types for file upload state management, including file metadata, extracted test data, and upload status tracking.
frontend/src/types/medicalRecords.ts: Defines TypeScript interfaces for all medical record data types (CBC, LFT, Cholesterol, etc.) along with reference ranges, field definitions, and display labels/colors per record type.
frontend/src/utils/recordSummary.ts: Utility functions to count records by category, find the latest date per category, and produce sorted category summary objects from a list of parsed medical records.
frontend/src/utils/trendAnalysis.ts: Provides trend analysis utilities including slope computation, improving/worsening direction detection, chart data building, and per-category metric key definitions for health records.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Utility functions for reading, storing, and clearing URL parameters from both query strings and hash-based routing, with sessionStorage persistence support.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration for the frontend, extending the default theme with custom colors (using oklch variables), fonts, border radii, animations, and plugins including typography, container queries, and tailwindcss-animate. Enables dark mode via class and scopes content to src files.
project_state.json: Project state manifest (version 7) describing a web-based medical records tracker built with React and Motoko, listing existing features, architectural notes, and requirements for CBC/LFT/Cholesterol/Sugar/BP record tracking with AI analysis, user authentication, OCR uploads, and a persistent dashboard panel.
